title: Pottery & Pause
shortDescription: Slow hand-building, soft light, and room to breathe between every coil.
description: An unhurried evening of clay, touch, and quiet conversation. We begin with a grounding pause, move through guided hand-building, and end with tea as pieces rest for firing. No prior experience needed—only curiosity and the willingness to soften your pace.

host.name: Ira Menon
host.bio: Ira’s practice sits between functional ware and contemplative making. She hosts sessions that privilege patience over perfection.

title: Golden Hour Painting Circle
shortDescription: Acrylics, warm light, and a collective hush as Mumbai turns amber.
description: Paint as the city exhales. We supply materials, gentle prompts, and a soundtrack of soft vinyl. The goal is not a finished masterpiece but a felt sense of color, distance, and presence. Suitable for beginners and returning creatives.

host.name: Devika Rao
host.bio: Devika’s sessions blend observational exercises with intuitive mark-making—always with room for silence.

title: Journal & Gentle Witness
shortDescription: Guided journaling, shared listening, and the quiet courage of being seen.
description: A circle for words on paper and words in the room—lightly held. Prompts invite memory, gratitude, and gentle edges. We close with an optional share; silence is always welcome. Bring your own notebook or use ours.

host.name: Noor Sheikh
host.bio: Noor designs containers for honest feeling without forcing disclosure—structure with softness.

title: Community Table: Creative Meetup
shortDescription: Strangers become collaborators over chai, sketches, and slow introductions.
description: An open table for illustrators, poets, designers, and the creatively curious. Short intros, a collaborative prompt, and plenty of unstructured time. We keep phones away for the first hour so gazes can actually meet.

host.name: The Pause Project hosts
host.bio: Our hosts rotate weekly—artists, therapists-in-training, and longtime members who know how to hold a room.

title: Stillness & Mark-Making
shortDescription: Breath, charcoal, and ink—art therapy–inspired, non-clinical, deeply human.
description: Inspired by art therapy principles but not a substitute for clinical care. We pair short mindfulness with expressive drawing exercises. Emphasis on process, metaphor, and self-compassion. Facilitators are trained in holding emotional safety.

host.name: Dr. Meera Kulkarni
host.bio: Meera integrates somatic pauses with visual expression for groups seeking gentle depth.
